title: Secure Communication
description: Review HTTPS, CORS configuration, and API security for Angular + .NET apps.
checklist: Explain why HTTPS is required for secure data exchange
Describe how CORS is configured in .NET Core
Show how Angular calls an API over a secure connection
Mention the role of authentication tokens or cookies
Summarize why backend and frontend must agree on security settings
answerHtml:
Sub QuestionAnswerMore ExplanationExampleBenefit
Why use HTTPS?Encrypts data over the wirePrevents eavesdropping and tamperinghttps://api.example.comProtects sensitive information
What is CORS?Browser policy for cross-origin requestsServer must explicitly allow trusted originsapp.UseCors("AllowSpecificOrigins")Prevents unauthorized API access
How does Angular call a secure API?Use HttpClient with https endpointBackend should serve TLS and return CORS headersthis.http.get('https://api.example.com') Safe front-end/back-end communication
What are auth tokens?Credentials sent with requestsJWT or cookies prove user identity to the APIAuthorization: Bearer tokenSecures each request
Why align backend and frontend security?Both sides must trust and authenticate requestsAPI restrictions and origin rules must match client behaviorAllow origins + secure headersReliable end-to-end security
